purpose: Evaluate every Bears plugin change against agent simplification, token economy, deterministic validation, and future reuse.

principles.rule: A governance change must make future agent work simpler through a shorter decision path, fewer repeated reads, or a clearer handoff.
A governance change must reduce repeated context load or justify any token-budget increase with a named payoff.
A governance change must name the files, packets, or summaries needed for the next worker and exclude broad content collection.
A governance change must add or update a deterministic validation path when it creates a durable rule.
A governance change must identify where future agents reuse the rule without reading the original issue or chat.
A governance change must state which decisions remain operator-owned and which checks are agent-owned.
A governance change must state when normal, no-subagent, subagent, audit, or validation mode applies.
A governance change must support compact handoff with status, scope, validation, and unresolved decision data.
A governance change must not add a gate, document, role, or checklist unless the payoff is explicit.

boundary_checks.single_bears_plugin: The Bears workflow governance model has one Codex plugin root at /srv/bears/plugins/bears.
boundary_checks.no_runtime_surface: This plugin must not add apps, connectors, MCP servers, runtime services, product behavior, or production mutation paths.
boundary_checks.external_speckit_boundary: Upstream Spec Kit remains external; this plugin may keep only Bears-owned governance skills, catalogs, validators, workflows, agents, capabilities, docs, templates, and tests.
boundary_checks.exact_role_coverage: Implementation may start only after the exact child target routes to one primary specialist or helper role at the same granularity as the write scope.
boundary_checks.english_entity_bound_artifacts: Artifacts and subagent messages must use English, strict concise wording, and exact entity names.
boundary_checks.restricted_data_exclusion: The change must not read, print, store, commit, or document secrets, credentials, private keys, .env values, production data, raw logs, shell history, or raw VPN configs.
boundary_checks.inventory_sync: Catalog, validator, README inventory, manifest, capability inventory, docs, and tests must stay synchronized when governance claims change.
boundary_checks.capability_inventory_boundary: The capabilities/ directory is executable governance inventory only: metadata, schemas, fixtures, wrappers, and validation evidence without apps, connectors, MCP servers, runtime services, product behavior, production mutation paths, or Codex environment mutation.

blocked_patterns.pattern: Creating another Bears governance plugin or layer outside /srv/bears/plugins/bears.
Adding product app, connector, MCP server, runtime service, product behavior, or production mutation behavior in this plugin.
Copying upstream Spec Kit skills into this plugin instead of calling the external source.
Using generic deploy wording when the entity is local_cd or kubernetes_deployment.
Adding sample, example, or illustrative policy sections to governed policy surfaces.
Reading, printing, storing, committing, or documenting restricted data or value-bearing credential material.

agentic_enterprise_constitution.rule: Bears plugin changes that affect L1/L2/L3 orchestration, hooks, scope matrices, decision logs, owner lineage, or four-domain boundaries must satisfy the agentic enterprise constitution.

surface_policy.rule: The plugin root may carry governance layers only. Product app, connector, MCP server, runtime service, service registration, and deploy registration surfaces are forbidden as paths or manifest keys.
